title: RoE plot change OR a new fact to the original NGE
display_title: RoE plot change OR a new fact to the original NGE
wikitext:
[]: RoE plot change OR a new fact to the original NGE?             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 11, 2008 6:39 pm So I watched the RoE again and started thinking.... Hmmmmmmmm..... Seele's base on the moon..... Hmmmm.... with unknown number of coffins, some opened, some closed. Kaworu is in one of them giving an impression that all the remaing angels are on the moon and the already killed came from the moon. Hmmm... This means Seele are in possesion of all the angels and they are the ones attacking Nerv, which is under their direct control. So they are fighting themselves using angels.... Hmmmmmm.... It looks like a HUGE plot change.... But what if it's indeed retelling like the authors say? Let's keep in mind that it was never cleared where the angels come from and what are their motives in NGE! Therefore! I dare You all! Answer my question and fanwank as much as You want ;) What if all the angels, and not only Kaworu, were kept and sent to action by Seele in the original Eva?? How does such knowledge change the original series? What theories it may backup? I may start and say that this RoE fact applied to the original NGE answers many questions, like why different angels seemed to attack different things throughout the series. It was because Seele marked their targets and times to attack! We couldn't pinpoint their aims, because we were looking in the wrong direction! Now we know why Gaghiel attacked the fleet in the 8th episode. Seele wanted to show the world, and the Eva opponents such as the captain of the aircraft, how useless standard weaponry is. Also, now we know it wasn't a coincidence that Matarael attacked during the powercut in the Geofront, and again it's obvious now why all the angels didn't attack at the same time and what's more important why they were giving enough time for NERV to restore it's fighting ability after every battle. Hmmmm... Last one question. What sub-forum should this thread be in? lol p.s. It's the first thread made by me. Don't be harsh, please. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 11, 2008 6:44 pm For the moon giant topic, you should consult the Moon Giant Mystery thread. Other than that the questions in this thread seem fine, but that is up for admins and mods to determine, of course.

[]: Re: RoE plot change OR a new fact to the original NGE?             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 11, 2008 6:49 pm simon wrote: Last one question. What sub-forum should this thread be in? lol

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Aug 11, 2008 7:55 pm Um no. That is way to contrived. SEELE cutting NERV's power and sending an Angel while they can't defend ensuring 3I happens even though they want it stopped? Them being surprised Ireul got into NERV? Sandalphon being in a volcano? Bardiel and Zeruel attacking in back to back days? How does this help them? How about the fact they called Sachiel a "disaster"? COMMITTEEMAN A: So, the Angels have returned, eh? It's so sudden. COMMITTEEMAN B: It's the same as it was fifteen years ago. A disaster comes as it will, without warning.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 11, 2008 8:46 pm Your theory contradicts known facts, especially if you take the Classified Information into consideration.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 12, 2008 1:04 am The original plot outline had this Episode 24: The promised time, now: Rei is defeated. Her secret is revealed. The most powerful, 12th Angel awakens, at last, and decends to Earth from the Moon. The Americas, along with the Eva unit-06 is erased from the surface of the Earth. Humanity learns futility against the Angel's absolute power. The promised time, when humanity will return to nothing, approaches. A human drama of hopelessness.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Aug 12, 2008 1:05 am Where the hell did you get that from!?!?!?!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 12, 2008 1:24 am Indirectly from the 100% Newtype Collection. The full text, copy-pasta'd from the forum post where I first saw it is here: http://www.windsong.demon.co.uk/nge/manyworlds.html

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:12 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Um no. That is way to contrived. SEELE cutting NERV's power and sending an Angel while they can't defend ensuring 3I happens even though they want it stopped? Them being surprised Ireul got into NERV? Sandalphon being in a volcano? Bardiel and Zeruel attacking in back to back days? How does this help them? How about the fact they called Sachiel a "disaster"? COMMITTEEMAN A: So, the Angels have returned, eh? It's so sudden. COMMITTEEMAN B: It's the same as it was fifteen years ago. A disaster comes as it will, without warning.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Aug 12, 2008 12:33 pm The problem with quoting the CI is that some of it contradicts with itself. There are only two Angels I can imagine SEELE sending intentionally other than Kaworu, and that's Iruel and Bardiel. However, I feel it's not that they sent them intentionally, it's just that they knew they were coming. The order of the Angels would be predicted in the Dead Sea Scrolls, I think, so, they would know an Angel had entered NERV either by prediction, a spy (Hi there Kaji), or a combination of the 2. Gaghiel was going after Adam, most likely. This is what I believe. The reason NERV got it's power cut is probably exactly as Ritsuko said, so someone could map out the systems by order of importance, or something like that. This would be useful information to SEELE (assuming they did it) considering they were drawing up plans to attack NERV. The claim from Khara was that this would be sticking more to the original plan than the series. If SEELE really had a moonbase, I think they'd have been able to retrieve the Spear of Longinus more directly. Or SEELE didn't want to tip their hand, you never know, but, I don't buy that SEELE sent all the Angels. I think they deliberately created or allowed the situations by which some of them (other than Kaworu) came. The reason they had tried to guide Kaworu was because he was the final Angel, and they wanted their plans to go through. I don't think it's applicable. Probably just about nothing new in Rebuild will be applicable, but I will not rule it out.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Tue Aug 12, 2008 12:51 pm cat42 wrote: If SEELE really had a moonbase, I think they'd have been able to retrieve the Spear of Longinus more directly.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:10 pm cat42 wrote: The problem with quoting the CI is that some of it contradicts with itself.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Aug 12, 2008 11:55 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: cat42 wrote: The problem with quoting the CI is that some of it contradicts with itself.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:29 am That discrepancy can be easily explained. Like Kaworu, Angels can't tell the difference between Seeds of Life.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:31 am AuraTwilight wrote: That discrepancy can be easily explained. Like Kaworu, Angels can't tell the difference between Seeds of Life.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 13, 2008 3:43 am Just because they're attracted to Mommy doesn't mean it's a conscious action. They might have no idea they're heading towards Adam, but go there anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Aug 13, 2008 3:50 am AuraTwilight wrote: Just because they're attracted to Mommy doesn't mean it's a conscious action. They might have no idea they're heading towards Adam, but go there anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Aug 13, 2008 4:54 am AuraTwilight wrote: Just because they're attracted to Mommy doesn't mean it's a conscious action. They might have no idea they're heading towards Adam, but go there anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Wed Aug 13, 2008 5:40 am master_lloyd wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Just because they're attracted to Mommy doesn't mean it's a conscious action. They might have no idea they're heading towards Adam, but go there anyway.

 someone could map out the systems by order of importance, or something like that.
